title: equiv_make is confused by feedback through module port
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Run the following script:
```
read_ilang bug.il
equiv_make gold gate equiv
hierarchy -top equiv
techmap -autoproc -map +/simcells.v
equiv_induct
equiv_status -assert
```
<details>
<summary>bug.il</summary>

```verilog
module \gold
  wire output 2 \o
  wire input 1 \sys_clk
  attribute \init 3'001
  wire \v
  wire \v$next
  wire input 3 \x
  wire input 4 \y
  cell $_MUX_ \_0_
    connect \A \o
    connect \B \x
    connect \S \y
    connect \Y \v$next
  end
  cell $_DFF_P_ \_1_
    connect \C \sys_clk
    connect \D \v$next
    connect \Q \o
  end
  connect \v \o
end
module \gate
  wire $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
  wire output 2 \o
  wire input 1 \sys_clk
  attribute \init 3'001
  wire \v
  wire \v$next
  wire input 3 \x
  wire input 4 \y
  cell $_XOR_ $auto$simplemap.cc:85:simplemap_bitop$5127
    connect \A \y
    connect \B 1'0
    connect \Y $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
  end
  cell $_MUX_ \_0_
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B \x
    connect \S \y
    connect \Y \v$next
  end
  cell $_DFFE_PP_ \_1_
    connect \C \sys_clk
    connect \D \v$next
    connect \E $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
    connect \Q \o
  end
  connect \v \o
end
```
</details>

The `gate` module was produced from `gold` module by running `dff2dffe -direct-match $_DFF_*`.

## Expected behavior

Equivalence check succeeds.

## Actual behavior

Equivalence check fails.

My understanding of this is as follows. Here are the `gold` and `gate` modules:

![screenshot_20181207_181930](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/49665577-31385000-fa4d-11e8-8e3a-a569ac97763a.png)
![screenshot_20181207_181944](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/49665581-339aaa00-fa4d-11e8-8fd3-c233145944a6.png)

When combined, it looks like `equiv_make` is confused by module ports, and wires one of the `$equiv` cells as an input to another `$equiv` cell via a combinatorial network. This probably breaks it:

![screenshot_20181207_182015](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54771/49665583-36959a80-fa4d-11e8-9fc3-19e09353fa1d.png)
code_blocks:
read_ilang bug.il
equiv_make gold gate equiv
hierarchy -top equiv
techmap -autoproc -map +/simcells.v
equiv_induct
equiv_status -assert

module \gold
  wire output 2 \o
  wire input 1 \sys_clk
  attribute \init 3'001
  wire \v
  wire \v$next
  wire input 3 \x
  wire input 4 \y
  cell $_MUX_ \_0_
    connect \A \o
    connect \B \x
    connect \S \y
    connect \Y \v$next
  end
  cell $_DFF_P_ \_1_
    connect \C \sys_clk
    connect \D \v$next
    connect \Q \o
  end
  connect \v \o
end
module \gate
  wire $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
  wire output 2 \o
  wire input 1 \sys_clk
  attribute \init 3'001
  wire \v
  wire \v$next
  wire input 3 \x
  wire input 4 \y
  cell $_XOR_ $auto$simplemap.cc:85:simplemap_bitop$5127
    connect \A \y
    connect \B 1'0
    connect \Y $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
  end
  cell $_MUX_ \_0_
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B \x
    connect \S \y
    connect \Y \v$next
  end
  cell $_DFFE_PP_ \_1_
    connect \C \sys_clk
    connect \D \v$next
    connect \E $auto$dff2dffe.cc:158:make_patterns_logic$5123
    connect \Q \o
  end
  connect \v \o
end
